forecasts.statement: At least one announced US AI data-center project rated 1 GW or larger will be publicly delayed, downsized, or cancelled with grid-interconnection or power-supply as a primary cited cause.
forecasts.resolution_criteria: A company statement, utility/regulatory filing, or report from a major outlet (Reuters/Bloomberg/WSJ/FT) documents a US data-center project of ≥1 GW planned capacity delayed, reduced in scope, or cancelled, with power availability, grid interconnection, or transmission explicitly cited as a primary reason. Routine schedule slips with no power rationale do not count.
forecasts.rationale: Physical inputs — not intelligence — are the binding constraint. Interconnection queues (multi-year) and regional power scarcity are already the gating factor for hyperscale buildout, and the sheer number of >=1GW announcements makes at least one power-driven stumble near-inevitable in the window.
forecasts.why_kept: Cleanest, highest-confidence expression of the energy-bind pillar with a hard capacity threshold; anchors the submission with a well-justified extreme probability.
forecasts.competition_strength: Strongly calibrated high-conviction call (0.87) with an unambiguous numeric trigger; demonstrates the thesis's core 'energy gates AI' claim without hedging.

forecasts.statement: The cheapest API price for a model matching or exceeding January-2026 GPT-4-class capability will be at least 90% lower than the cheapest such option available in January 2026.
forecasts.resolution_criteria: Published per-million-token pricing for any commercially available model that matches or exceeds a fixed Jan-2026 GPT-4-class reference score on a standard public benchmark (e.g., MMLU or GPQA) is ≥90% below the lowest-priced qualifying model as of January 2026.
forecasts.rationale: Capability diffuses without a durable moat and the model layer commoditizes; prior GPT-4-class price curves already fell >90% within ~18 months, so a 3-year window makes this near-structural rather than speculative.
forecasts.why_kept: The load-bearing evidence for 'rent leaks out of the model layer'; fully objective (published price vs fixed benchmark) and pairs with F10 to show where the rent goes.
forecasts.competition_strength: High calibration on a mechanism with strong historical precedent; the fixed-benchmark clause removes moving-goalpost ambiguity.

forecasts.statement: At least one frontier-scale AI compute deployment of 200 MW-equivalent or larger will operate primarily on off-grid dedicated generation, offshore siting, or satellite-integrated architecture rather than standard utility interconnection.
forecasts.resolution_criteria: Credible reporting or company disclosure confirms an operational AI compute deployment drawing ≥200 MW whose primary power/siting is behind-the-meter dedicated generation islanded from the grid, offshore, or space/satellite-integrated — i.e., not dependent on standard RTO/ISO grid interconnection.
forecasts.rationale: The space/off-grid compute modality routes physical buildout around both the interconnection bottleneck and the regulable grid, giving the West's siting/export-control containment tools a built-in expiry date. Behind-the-meter gas at this scale is already emerging, and the criterion resolves on operational fact rather than stated intent.
forecasts.why_kept: The originality centerpiece — the proof-of-concept that effective control migrates off the regulable grid. No other kept forecast captures the jurisdiction-agnostic wildcard.
forecasts.competition_strength: Most original/contrarian pick; reframes containment as time-limited. Intent language stripped so it resolves on an observable capacity fact.

forecasts.statement: In at least one calendar year, credible reporting will document that 100,000 or more restricted advanced AI accelerators (H100-class or better) reached China despite US export controls.
forecasts.resolution_criteria: A US/allied government report, GAO/BIS/congressional finding, or investigative report by a major outlet quantifies ≥100,000 export-restricted advanced accelerators entering China in a single calendar year via smuggling, transshipment, or offshore cloud circumvention.
forecasts.rationale: Export controls are a real chokepoint but leak from within, and they target the silicon layer where durable value no longer sits. The lever erodes faster than it binds — the mercantilist tool degrades on its own timeline.
forecasts.why_kept: Mercantilist lever (export controls) plus a contrarian rebuttal of 'controls work'; distinct from F4's off-grid mechanism (this is diversion of restricted silicon, not siting).
forecasts.competition_strength: Contrarian and mercantilist; the numeric threshold and named report-types give a scoreable bar, with probability moderated for reporting-precision risk.

forecasts.statement: US employment in at least one major AI-exposed white-collar occupation (software developers, paralegals/legal assistants, or customer-service representatives) will fall 10% or more below its 2024–2025 peak.
forecasts.resolution_criteria: US BLS data (OES or CES occupational series) shows the employment level for at least one of the three named occupations at least 10% below its highest 2024–2025 annual reading.
forecasts.rationale: Agentic capability substitutes for tasks faster than it reinstates complementary ones; the thesis holds that AI displaces pervasively even without a decisive lead. A 10% decline in a single most-exposed category is a moderate, not maximal, claim.
forecasts.why_kept: The only labor-displacement forecast; expresses 'enriches and displaces' and is fully objective via named BLS series.
forecasts.competition_strength: Objective official-statistics anchor; the named occupation set and fixed peak baseline prevent cherry-picking, and 0.55 reflects genuine two-sided uncertainty.

forecasts.statement: The US real policy rate (effective fed funds minus 12-month headline CPI) will be negative for at least six consecutive months.
forecasts.resolution_criteria: Monthly effective federal funds rate minus year-over-year headline CPI is below zero for six or more consecutive calendar months, per Fed (FRED series DFF) and BLS CPI data.
forecasts.rationale: Fiscal dominance and eroding central-bank independence tilt the regime toward financial repression — negative real rates as the debt-management tool. This is currency/financial statecraft, the macro face of mercantilism.
forecasts.why_kept: Mercantilist lever (financial repression) and the macro-regime pillar; resolvable to the month from two public series. Chosen over F9 to avoid a second, conditional macro forecast.
forecasts.competition_strength: Precise, machine-checkable resolution from FRED/BLS; expresses fiscal dominance without vague 'inflation stays high' language.

forecasts.statement: At least one independent top-tier frontier AI lab (OpenAI, Anthropic, xAI, Mistral, or comparable) will be acquired by or brought under majority/exclusive operational control of a hyperscaler or consumer-platform incumbent.
forecasts.resolution_criteria: A named independent frontier lab becomes the subject of a completed acquisition, a majority equity stake, or a contractual arrangement granting a hyperscaler/consumer-platform incumbent exclusive control of its compute, model IP, and go-to-market — confirmed by company announcement or regulatory filing.
forecasts.rationale: Rent migrates up-stack to distribution; pure model labs lack a standalone moat once capability commoditizes (see F2) and get pulled into platform incumbents that own users, power, and channels. 'Who controls platforms' beats 'who reaches AGI'.
forecasts.why_kept: Names where the rent goes (distribution), complementing F2's 'rent leaves the model layer'; contrarian against the 'labs are permanent kingmakers' consensus.
forecasts.competition_strength: Contrarian and directly tied to the thesis's platform-control claim; the acquisition/majority/exclusive-control triple keeps resolution objective.

forecasts.statement: China's PLA will initiate a declared military blockade, quarantine, or direct kinetic action against Taiwan beyond routine exercises.
forecasts.resolution_criteria: Official statements or consensus major-outlet reporting confirm a PLA-declared blockade/quarantine of Taiwan or a direct kinetic strike on Taiwan-controlled territory. Routine drills, ADIZ incursions, and gray-zone coercion do not count.
forecasts.rationale: Beijing's Taiwan calculus is driven by politics, not economic interdependence, so the kinetic tail is live and not defused by trade linkage — but the base rate over three years is low. The calibrated low number is the point: a real but non-modal tail on the compute-chokepoint container.
forecasts.why_kept: The 'live Taiwan tail' is explicit in the thesis; the deliberately low probability demonstrates calibration discipline rather than 50/50 hedging.
forecasts.competition_strength: Well-justified extreme (<0.20); the exclusion of routine drills makes an otherwise fuzzy geopolitical event cleanly scoreable.

forecasts.statement: At least 3 of the 20 most populous non-treaty-aligned 'swing' states will adopt a predominantly Chinese-origin AI stack (Chinese frontier models and/or Chinese-made accelerators) for state or critical-infrastructure deployments.
forecasts.resolution_criteria: Documented procurement/deployment (government contract, state media, or major-outlet reporting) in ≥3 qualifying swing states where Chinese-origin frontier models or Chinese-made accelerators are the primary stack for a state or critical-infrastructure system.
forecasts.rationale: Fragmentation gravitates toward two compute stacks with no stable middle; as US controls leak and Chinese offerings mature, swing states are pushed to pick, and price/access advantage tilts several toward the Chinese stack.
forecasts.why_kept: Mercantilist lever (bloc/supply-chain formation) and the clearest 'two-stack world' forecast; distinct from F5/F15 which are about leakage and trade volume respectively.
forecasts.competition_strength: Captures unstable multipolarity with a countable threshold; probability reflects genuine uncertainty over adoption pace, avoiding overconfidence.

forecasts.statement: Full-year 2028 US–China two-way goods trade in nominal USD will remain at 80% or more of full-year 2024 levels.
forecasts.resolution_criteria: Official US Census Bureau and/or China Customs statistics show 2028 total US–China two-way goods trade in nominal USD at ≥80% of the 2024 total.
forecasts.rationale: The trade 'breakdown' is institutional/rule erosion layered atop still-flowing physical trade; tariffs and export rules fray the framework while volumes prove sticky. China reads more as an economic rebalancer than a clean decoupler.
forecasts.why_kept: Mercantilist lever (tariffs/trade) and a sharply contrarian counter to the decoupling narrative; the nominal-USD floor is directly measurable from official stats.
forecasts.competition_strength: Contrarian against consensus decoupling, resolvable to a single ratio from official data — a defensible roundtable centerpiece on 'rules erode but flows persist'.

dropped.reason: Overlaps F4 as the space-compute node; a single-site GPU count is harder to verify and less expressive of the jurisdiction-bypass claim than F4's off-grid capacity criterion.
Institutional-erosion pillar already carried by F5 and F8; 'materially weaken/broad exemption' invites judgment calls, failing the objective-resolution bar.
Duplicates F8 on the macro/fiscal-dominance pillar and is conditional on a ≥20% drawdown occurring, adding resolution fragility for no new theme.
Near-duplicate of F1 on the energy-bind theme; kept the sharper project-cancellation version to preserve breadth across the ten.
Genuine thesis point (no decisive lead) but reconstructing a '12-month lead over the 5th-ranked model' is too subjective to score cleanly; commoditization is captured objectively by F2.

submission_note: The ten forecasts trace the worldview end to end: energy and jurisdiction — not intelligence — bind AI power (F1 grid stall, F4 off-grid/space compute routing around the regulable grid); rent leaks out of the commoditizing model layer (F2) and migrates up-stack to distribution incumbents (F10); Western institutions and containment tools erode faster than they bind (F5 export-control leakage, F8 financial repression); the technology displaces labor pervasively while handing no one a decisive lead (F7); and the container fragments into two compute stacks with a live kinetic tail and sticky physical trade (F11 Taiwan, F12 stack bloc, F15 US–China trade). Four forecasts (F5, F8, F12, F15) turn on named mercantilist levers — export controls, financial statecraft, bloc formation, and tariffs/trade — satisfying the brief's mandatory mercantilism coverage. Four are deliberately contrarian (controls leak, labs get absorbed, compute goes off-grid, trade persists despite decoupling), and probabilities span a calibrated range from 0.12 to 0.88 rather than clustering at 50/50.
